sanskrit: अमी च त्वां धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्राः सर्वे सहैवावनिपालसङ्घैः । भीष्मो द्रोणः सूतपुत्रस्तथासौ सहास्मदीयैरपि योधमुख्यैः ॥ २७ ॥
transliteration: amī ca tvāṃ dhṛtarāṣṭrasya putrāḥ sarve sahaivāvani-pāla-saṅghaiḥ / bhīṣmo droṇaḥ sūta-putras tathāsau sahāsmadīyair api yodha-mukhyaiḥ
literal_meaning: These—Dhritarāshtra’s sons, all, together with hosts of warrior-kings, Bhishma, Drona, the charioteer’s son (Karṇa), likewise, along with our own front-rank fighters—are rushing into Your mouths, their heads smashed by Your terrible teeth; some are seen stuck between the teeth, their skulls ground to powder.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna sees that every figure he has idealized or feared—enemy heroes, beloved elders, even his own comrades—is being devoured by Time. The vision collapses the split between ‘good’ and ‘bad’ people; all are equally absorbed into a single cosmic process. Psychologically, this is the moment when the ego’s selective attachments are shattered and the Self confronts the archetype of inevitable annihilation.
psychological_state: Acute derealization: the familiar world is suddenly perceived as a slaughter-field inside a single cosmic mouth; personal identity feels porous, heroic narratives meaningless.
trigger_scenario: A war-veteran with PTSD scrolling through drone-cam footage sees friends and foes alike disappear into a fireball; or an ICU nurse during a mass-casualty event watching ventilators claim both the ‘deserving’ and the ‘undeserving’ without distinction.
anchor_text: Everyone you love, fear, or fight is already being swallowed by time—let this dissolve your clinging.
